name: Differential Evolution
key_facts: Differential Evolution (DE) is a population-based stochastic optimization algorithm for continuous spaces
It uses vector differences between population members for perturbation (mutation)
Key operations are mutation, crossover (binomial or exponential), and selection
DE was introduced by Storn and Price in 1997
Control parameters include the mutation factor (F), crossover rate (CR), and population size (NP)
DE is derivative-free and robust for noisy, multimodal, and non-differentiable objective functions
Common variants include DE/rand/1, DE/best/1, and DE/current-to-best/1
forbidden_claims: Differential Evolution guarantees the global optimum
Differential Evolution is only applicable to discrete optimization
Differential Evolution does not use a population
